title: SEC3: double-Rc wrapping in NieClient.inner
description: api.rs line 100: `inner: Rc<RefCell<Option<Rc<NieRelayClient>>>>`. There are two Rc layers. The outer `Rc` allows `&self` methods to clone a reference into async blocks. The inner `Rc<NieRelayClient>` is used so that `send_message` etc. can clone the client out of the RefCell before awaiting. However, `Rc::get_mut` in `on_event` (line 161) succeeds only when the inner Rc has exactly one reference — i.e., the same condition that would hold if it were a plain `Box`. The inner `Rc` only becomes useful if a second strong reference exists, but the invariant the code relies on (`get_mut` succeeding) requires there NOT to be a second reference. The inner Rc thus provides no benefit over `Box`, but adds complexity and the misleading implication that sharing is intended. Simplify to `Rc<RefCell<Option<NieRelayClient>>>` and make `NieRelayClient` fields that need cloning into async blocks use their own `Rc` internally (as `online_users` and `transport` already do).
close_reason: Simplified: NieRelayClient.transport is now Rc<WasmTransport>; NieRelayClient impls Clone (cheap Rc clones). inner is now Rc<RefCell<Option<NieRelayClient>>> — no double-Rc. on_event no longer needs Rc::get_mut.
